data.title: Iran Launches Second Wave of Missile Strikes Towards Israel
data.description: Iran announced on March 26 that it launched a second wave of missile strikes towards Israel, according to BlockBeats. This action follows an earlier missile attack on the same day. Air raid sirens were activated in several locations across Israel, including Tel Aviv, central regions, and Jerusalem, as a response to the missile threat.

data.title: Bain Capital Secures $299 Million Loan for Australian Acquisition
data.description: Bain Capital has obtained a $299 million leveraged loan to support its acquisition of the Australian wealth management division of Perpetual. Bloomberg posted on X, citing sources familiar with the transaction. This financial move marks a significant step in Bain Capital's expansion strategy in the region. The acquisition is expected to enhance Bain Capital's presence in the wealth management sector, aligning with its broader investment goals.

data.title: G7 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Convene, French Minister Says
data.description: France's Economy Minister announced that a meeting was convened on Monday with G7 finance ministers, energy ministers, and central bank governors. According to Jin10, the gathering aimed to address pressing economic and energy issues affecting the member countries. The discussions are expected to focus on collaborative strategies to tackle current global economic challenges.

data.title: Elon Musk Highlights Starlink's Potential Impact on National GDP
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). Elon Musk has expressed his belief that Starlink, the satellite internet constellation operated by SpaceX, could significantly boost a country's GDP. Musk argues that internet connectivity is one of the most effective ways to help people escape poverty. 

According to Musk, access to the internet allows individuals to tap into global information, take courses from institutions like MIT, and sell goods and services. Without affordable and reliable internet, these opportunities remain inaccessible. 

Musk emphasizes that GDP is a function of per capita productivity. Technologies like Starlink, which enhance individual productivity, can have a tangible impact on a nation's GDP.

data.title: Chainlink's Potential Recovery in April Amidst Declining Altcoin Trading Volume
data.description: Chainlink (LINK) may see a recovery in April as its price remains below $10. According to NS3.AI, U.S. spot LINK ETFs have amassed $93.74 million in net assets. However, the significant 85% drop in altcoin trading volume could pose a challenge to any potential upside for Chainlink.

data.title: Peter Schiff Predicts Stablecoin Interest Ban in the U.S.
data.description: Peter Schiff has expressed concerns about the strength of the U.S. banking lobby compared to the crypto sector. According to NS3.AI, Schiff predicts that stablecoin issuers will be prohibited from offering interest payments in the United States. He attributes this potential restriction to the stablecoin interest provisions included in the pending Clarity Act. Schiff also suggested that if such a rule is implemented, tokenized gold could see a positive impact.

data.title: France to Announce Measures to Mitigate High Oil Prices Impact, Says Economy Minister
data.description: France's Economy Minister has stated that new measures will be announced in the coming days to alleviate the impact of high oil prices. According to Jin10, the minister emphasized the government's commitment to addressing the economic challenges posed by rising energy costs. The announcement is expected to outline strategies aimed at stabilizing the market and supporting consumers and businesses affected by the price surge. The French government is actively seeking solutions to manage the economic repercussions and ensure stability in the energy sector.

data.title: ECB's Nagel: April Rate Hike Is an Option, But Not a Certainty
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member Joachim Nagel stated that a rate hike in April is a possibility, but not a certainty. According to Jin10, Nagel emphasized that while an increase in interest rates is on the table, it remains just one of several options being considered by the ECB. The decision will depend on economic data and the overall financial situation in the Eurozone. Nagel's comments highlight the ECB's cautious approach to monetary policy amid ongoing economic uncertainties.

data.title: Product Manager X: Grok will take full control of the recommendation algorithm next week.
data.description: Nikita Bier, product lead for X, announced on the X platform that Grok will take full control of the platform's recommendation algorithm next week, calling it one of the most significant changes in X's history. Previously, Elon Musk had announced a major update to the AI ​​recommendation system and the simultaneous open-sourcing of the code. The new algorithm, codenamed "Phoenix," is based on the Transformer architecture, uses an end-to-end learning approach, and is planned to be released on GitHub on the same day as its launch.

data.title: ECB Considers Rate Hike Amid Middle East Conflict Impact on Inflation
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) is contemplating a potential interest rate hike in response to escalating inflation driven by the Middle East conflict, according to ECB Governing Council member Nagel. The conflict in Iran has led to a surge in energy prices, prompting the ECB to consider raising rates. According to Jin10, investors are speculating whether the first rate hike will occur in April or June. Nagel stated that he and his colleagues will have sufficient information, including the war's economic impact, to decide on a rate hike at the meeting on April 29-30. "This is certainly an option, but just one of them," he added. "I believe by April, we will have enough data to determine whether action is necessary or if we can continue to wait. However, we cannot avoid a rate hike simply because we think the timing is premature."

data.title: Mapletree Investments to Liquidate Property Fund After Underperformance
data.description: Singapore's Mapletree Investments has announced plans to liquidate a property fund that previously managed assets exceeding $1.4 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the decision comes after the fund's investments in student dormitories experienced years of underperformance. The move reflects ongoing challenges in the real estate sector, particularly in niche markets like student housing, which have faced difficulties in recent years. Mapletree's decision underscores the broader trend of reevaluating investment strategies amid changing market conditions.

data.title: Thailand's Central Bank Maintains Policy Rate at 1%, Indicates Easing Stance
data.description: Thailand's central bank has announced that its current policy interest rate remains at 1%, reflecting a stance of monetary easing. According to Jin10, this decision aligns with the bank's ongoing efforts to support economic growth amid global uncertainties. The central bank's approach aims to stimulate domestic demand and maintain financial stability in the face of external economic pressures. The policy rate, which has been held steady, is part of a broader strategy to ensure that the country's economic recovery remains on track. The bank continues to monitor economic indicators closely to assess the need for any future adjustments.

data.title: Anthropic's Claude Code Integrates iMessage for Local Execution
data.description: Anthropic Claude Code team member Thariq Shihipar announced the integration of iMessage as a communication channel for Claude Code. According to Odaily, users can install a plugin on their Mac, allowing them to send instructions to a locally running Claude Code session via iPhone or other Apple devices. Claude executes these commands locally and returns results through iMessage. This functionality is achieved by reading the macOS Messages database and utilizing AppleScript, without relying on external services. Currently in the research preview stage, it requires Claude Code version 2.1.80 or higher for support.

data.title: Honda Bolivia Launches Polygon Payment Initiative with Takenos
data.description: Honda's Bolivia distributor, Autobol, has partnered with digital wallet Takenos to introduce a Polygon payment campaign in Bolivia. According to Foresight News, the first 200 customers who use Takenos to pay for preventive vehicle maintenance will receive a discount of 200 Bolivianos.

data.title: Philippines Halts Wholesale Electricity Spot Market Amid Energy Concerns
data.description: The Philippines has temporarily suspended its wholesale electricity spot market as of Thursday, implementing emergency measures to tackle ongoing energy supply challenges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of a broader strategy to stabilize the country's energy sector. The suspension aims to mitigate risks associated with energy shortages and ensure a more reliable power supply. This decision reflects the government's proactive approach to addressing potential disruptions in the energy market.

data.title: Chinese Firms Maintain Presence in Arabian Gulf Amid Regional Turmoil
data.description: Chinese companies are continuing their operations in the Arabian Gulf despite ongoing regional instability, according to a leading management consultancy. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these firms remain committed to their investments and partnerships in the area. The consultancy noted that the strategic importance of the Gulf region, coupled with its economic potential, makes it a vital area for Chinese businesses. Despite challenges, these companies are focused on long-term growth and are adapting to the changing geopolitical landscape. The report underscores the resilience and strategic foresight of Chinese enterprises in navigating complex international environments.

data.title: Thailand's Central Bank: Inflation Below Target Does Not Indicate Deflation
data.description: Thailand's central bank has stated that inflation rates falling below the target level should not be interpreted as deflation. According to Jin10, the bank emphasized that the current economic conditions do not suggest a deflationary trend, despite inflation being lower than anticipated. The central bank reassured that the economy remains stable and is not experiencing the negative impacts typically associated with deflation. This statement comes amid ongoing discussions about inflation rates and their implications for the country's economic health.

data.title: Qantas Adjusts Fleet to Optimize Capacity
data.description: Qantas has announced a strategic adjustment in its fleet operations to optimize capacity. According to Jin10, the airline is reallocating some of its Boeing 787 aircraft from U.S. routes and converting certain domestic A330 flights to international routes. This move is part of Qantas's efforts to enhance operational efficiency and better meet demand across its network.

data.title: Australia Sees Increased Interest from France in Critical Mineral Projects
data.description: Australia's Resources Minister Madeleine announced on Thursday that France is among the countries preparing to invest in Australia's critical mineral projects. According to Jin10, this development follows a framework agreement signed between Canberra and the United States, which encourages countries with advanced manufacturing sectors to secure supplies of essential minerals. Australia has initiated a four-year plan to establish industries for minerals like rare earths, which are crucial for future technologies in electronics and defense. Madeleine noted that since the agreement with the U.S., other partners have accelerated their efforts to ensure access to these critical minerals. "France is becoming increasingly active," she stated.

data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Futures Rise Over $2 Amid Middle East Conflict Concerns
data.description: U.S. crude oil futures have increased by more than $2 per barrel, reaching $92.38, driven by concerns over the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. According to Jin10, the market is reacting to fears that the situation may persist, potentially impacting global oil supply. Traders are closely monitoring developments in the region, as any escalation could further influence oil prices. The current price movement reflects heightened market sensitivity to geopolitical tensions, with investors seeking to understand the potential long-term effects on the energy sector.
